original: The switchboard contains a number of buttons, or switches, in either one of two positions. This one here is in the On position.

original: And this one here is in the Off position. Simple so far, right?

original: Now, when you press on a switch, it will flip positions--but it will also toggle the positions of the four directly-adjacent switches as well!
context: <<POS:12795547>> });var l=o.prepHelper();l.text='Now, when you press on a switch, it will flip positions--but'

original: Ultimately, your objective is to “clear” the board by having all switches in the same position. You can find the target condition under Goal. In this case, the solution requires all Lights On.
context: <<POS:12796073>> });var c=o.prepHelper();c.text='Ultimately, your objective is to “clear” the board by having'

original: In addition, not all switchboards come with just one condition. Sometimes they can come with a Fail state--for this, the board will crash with all Lights Off.
context: <<POS:12796709>> });var g=o.prepHelper();g.text='In addition, not all switchboards come with just one conditi'

original: There are also trickier boards that track your Moves Left, so you’ll only have a limited number of actions to solve those...
context: <<POS:12797313>> });var y=o.prepHelper();y.text='There are also trickier boards that track your <b>Moves Left'

original: While a board can be solid enough to take as many solutions you can throw at it, some are more sensitive and may become unsuable if you fail too many times. For those boards, you’ll see the amount of Attempts Left.
context: <<POS:12797893>> });var f=o.prepHelper();f.text='While a board can be solid enough to take as many solutions '
